title: Episode 8 and EoE
wikitext:
[]: Episode 8 and EoE             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Fri Jul 04, 2014 6:18 pm In NGE Ep. 8: Asuka Strikes, Shinji joins Asuka inside EVA 02 using the female plugsuit along with the receptors (the objects they put in their heads) which after the Angel battle, Kensuke and Toji mocks Shinji for still wearing the female EVA 02 plugsuit. In EoE, when Shinji gets in the EVA 01, he just using the receptors while using his usual clothes. That leaves me four questions: - If Shinji was just using the receptors without the plugsuit in EoE, what are the plugsuits for? - Could Shinji refuse to wear the EVA 02 plugsuit and just put the receptors to get inside EVA 02 with Asuka? - Why the fuck Asuka wanted Shinji to get inside in the EVA 02 with her? - Wouldn't be easier if Asuka battled with the Angel alone and Shinji would watch the battle? P.S.: If I were Shinji I would tell Asuka to go fuck herself and watch the Angel battle in the carrier.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Jul 04, 2014 7:29 pm -The plugsuits help pilots to synchronize better with their EVAs and help absorb some of the rigors of damage and the like as well as helping monitor their biological functions. Remember, he also pilots sans plugsuit in ep 1, 2, and 19. They aren't necessary per se, but useful. -He very well could have, but was just complying because he's easily persuaded by such a forceful personality as Asuka's. -She was intrigued by him at that point and may have been using this opportunity to see what all the big fuss was about his piloting abilities. -Probably.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 04, 2014 8:26 pm - As Nacks said, Rebuild also added a defibrillator in the plugsuit - He could have, but better get all the advantages possible, and a plugsuit, even if not made for his body proportion, still offers a boost in synchronization. Besides Asuka is not the kind of person you say "no" very easily! And actually he wasn't even wearing the neural clips in EoE! They too offer a nice boost in synchronization, but the Eva can be piloted without them (although it's really not recommended) - She wanted to show off and show him "from the inside" how a true pilot works. Yes that was stupid, but Asuka tends to do very stupid things when her perceived pride is on the line. - Tied to the answer above, yes Asuka would had probably done better without Shinji's "mental pollution", unless Asuka is completely unable to make her Eva go berserk due to how closed her heart is, and Shinji being inside was actually the trigger for Kyoko losing her shit, in which case Shinji saved her life without any of them aware of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Jul 04, 2014 8:41 pm ElMariachi wrote: Shinji being inside was actually the trigger for Kyoko losing her shit, in which case Shinji saved her life without any of them aware of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Fri Jul 04, 2014 9:28 pm @ Guy Nacks and @ ElMariachi : Thanks for answering! Specially, ElMariachi for telling me this funny fact! They really were lucky EVA 02 didn't went ape shit... Asuka's attitude is one of my reasons I want to write a fic where Shinji becomes really different from his personality.

[]: Re: Episode 8 and EoE             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 04, 2014 9:47 pm Thomas68 wrote: ...the receptors (the objects they put in their heads)

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Fri Jul 04, 2014 9:50 pm Thanks, Eva Monkey! I didn't remember the name of those! Sorry for the mistake!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 04, 2014 9:59 pm Thomas68 wrote: Thanks, Eva Monkey! I didn't remember the name of those! Sorry for the mistake!

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Fri Jul 04, 2014 10:37 pm ElMariachi wrote: unless Asuka is completely unable to make her Eva go berserk due to how closed her heart is, and Shinji being inside was actually the trigger for Kyoko losing her shit, in which case Shinji saved her life without any of them aware of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jul 05, 2014 12:40 am BC Baron wrote: It's been a while since I've seen that episode, but if I'm remembering correctly, there was a brief scene toward the end where Ritsuko is talking to Misato and seems to suggest that the combination of the two pilots in Unit-02 may have briefly resulted in a higher sync ratio during the battle.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 05, 2014 12:57 am @ElMariachi The defibrilator function was already there in episode 6.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 05, 2014 1:00 am Monk Ed wrote: More than suggest, she said they broke their records -- both of them. For one brilliant moment, Shinji was able to synch with Kyoko better than he did with his own mother. I guess Kyoko really, really approved of their relationship.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 05, 2014 2:52 am Shinji being able to synch with Kyoko at all seems... problematic, to say the least. This means that either Shinji is somehow related to Kyoko biologically or else the entire mother/child thing is complete bullshit from the start. ...or perhaps it's because Kyoko's inability to recognize her own child now means that she can be piloted by any child? Could Rei pilot 02? Could Kensuke?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jul 05, 2014 5:41 am NemZ wrote: Shinji being able to synch with Kyoko at all seems... problematic, to say the least.

[]: Re: Episode 8 and EoE             Quote Post by EvaBrothers » Sat Jul 05, 2014 8:13 am Thomas68 wrote: Why the fuck Asuka wanted Shinji to get inside in the EVA 02 with her?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 05, 2014 12:39 pm Monk Ed wrote: Only if we adhere to unreasonable, nerd-like rigidity in our fictional mechanics.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jul 05, 2014 5:37 pm Chuckman wrote: This pleases Chuckman.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 05, 2014 9:37 pm Monk Ed wrote: unreasonable, nerd-like rigidity

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 05, 2014 10:21 pm It's called the willing suspension of disbelief. You gotta play ball a little and let things go otherwise you end up badgering people to find out how the heisenberg compensator works. (Very well, thank you)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 05, 2014 10:29 pm Shut up, Wesley.
